severity.description:
Severity of this diagnostic.

Possible values:

  • 0 - Information: Purely informational – the operation progressed normally.
  • 1 - Warning: The operation completed but with a potential issue that deserves attention.
  • 2 - Error: A recoverable error occurred; the operation may have partially succeeded.
  • 3 - Critical: An unrecoverable error; the operation failed entirely.

code.description:
A stable, machine-readable code that identifies the diagnostic category.
Examples: "ANALYSIS_CONVERGED", "MATERIAL_NOT_FOUND", "MESH_QUALITY_LOW".
An AI agent can use this code to decide on corrective actions.

source.description:
Optional – the API operation or domain area that produced this entry
(e.g. "Analysis", "Materials", "Sections", "Combinations").

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when the diagnostic was recorded.

DiagnosticEntry.description:
A single diagnostic message emitted during an API operation.
Designed for both human and AI consumption: the AD.API.Srv.Models.DiagnosticEntry.Code field
provides a stable, machine-readable identifier while AD.API.Srv.Models.DiagnosticEntry.Message
carries a human-readable explanation.

DiagnosticSeverity.description:
Severity level for a diagnostic entry produced during an API operation. Ordered by increasing severity so consumers can filter with simple comparisons.

Possible values:

  • 0 - Information: Purely informational – the operation progressed normally.
  • 1 - Warning: The operation completed but with a potential issue that deserves attention.
  • 2 - Error: A recoverable error occurred; the operation may have partially succeeded.
  • 3 - Critical: An unrecoverable error; the operation failed entirely.

logVerbosity.description:
Verbosity level for engine log output in API responses. Level 1: Errors only. Level 2: Errors and Warnings. Level 3: All details. Default is ErrorsAndWarnings (2).

Possible values:

  • 1 - Errors: Level 1: Only errors are included in the API response diagnostics.
  • 2 - ErrorsAndWarnings: Level 2: Errors and warnings are included in the API response diagnostics.
  • 3 - AllDetails: Level 3: All details (errors, warnings, and informational messages) are included.

GeneralBeamType_API.description:
General beam type

Possible values:

  • 0 - bar: Defines the types of linear finite element models supported in the system. This enumeration includes standard structural elements and composite beam types.
  • 0 - bar: Defines the types of linear finite element models supported in the system. This enumeration includes standard structural elements and composite beam types.
  • 1 - beamWStandardBending: Beam element - standard bending member
  • 1 - beamWStandardBending: Beam element - standard bending member
  • 2 - sbeam: S-beam element - beam with shear deformation
  • 2 - sbeam: S-beam element - beam with shear deformation
  • 3 - variablebeam: Variable beam element - beam with variable cross-section properties
  • 3 - variablebeam: Variable beam element - beam with variable cross-section properties
  • 4 - tie: Tie element - tension-only member
  • 4 - tie: Tie element - tension-only member
  • 5 - strut: Strut element - compression-only member
  • 5 - strut: Strut element - compression-only member
  • 6 - cable: Cable element - flexible tension member with sag behavior
  • 7 - rigid: Rigid element - infinitely stiff connection
  • 7 - rigid: Rigid element - infinitely stiff connection
  • 8 - CompositeBeamSimpleBeam: Composite beam with simple beam behavior
  • 9 - CompositeBeamSbeam: Composite beam with S-beam behavior (includes shear deformation)
  • 10 - CompositeBeamVariable: Composite beam with variable cross-section properties

Gutter_type.description:
Gutter types, Selection of whether to include the gutter effect. Gutter effect creates additional load accumulation when automatic generating snow loads, due to snow melting. The behavior is described in the art. 5.2(6) of NF EN 1991-1-3/NA

Possible values:

  • 0 - CG_GUTTER_EFFECT_NONE: no gutter effect
  • 1 - CG_GUTTER_EFFECT_STRONG: strong effect, used to simulate gutter on slopes with slope% < 3% [ degrees=arctangent(slope) ; for 3% slope = 0.03, for 100% slope = 1 ]

parapetReductionHeightByPurlinsAndRoofCo.description:

Unit: m

Default value : 0.0

snowGuardsPresence.description:
Use this option when snow fences or other obstructions exist on this load area or where the lower edge of the roof(load area) is terminated with a parapet. The snow load shape coefficient is then not reduced below 0,8 for monopitch and pitched roofs (5.3.2 (2), 5.3.3(2), EN 1991-1-3)

Default value : false

unobstructedSlippery.description:
Canada : unobstructed slippery roofs where snow and ice can slide completely off the roof

US : The ability to shed snow load by sliding (roof’s surface slipperiness).

A roof shall be considered unobstructed if no objects exist on it that prevent snow on it from sliding.

Slippery surfaces shall include metal, slate, glass, and bituminous, rubber, and plastic membranes with a smooth surface.

Default value : false

GammaDefavGEO = 1.35 (unfavourable permanent action GEO)

Set to null to allow engine defaults per national annex / standard.

Load_Area_Type.description:
Load area climatic type

Possible values:

  • 0 - CG_LOADAREA_BUILDING: building wall( >= GR_MIN_VERTICAL_WALL_ANGLE degrees) or roof( < GR_MIN_VERTICAL_WALL_ANGLE)
  • 1 - CG_LOADAREA_PROTRUDING_ROOF: protruding roof(avancees de toiture) ex awning(auvent) roof
  • 2 - CG_LOADAREA_PARAPET: parapet(acrotere) wall
  • 3 - CG_LOADAREA_ISOLATED_1_SLOPED_ROOF: isolated 1 sloped roof(1 slope canopy) (toiture isolee a 1 versant)
  • 4 - CG_LOADAREA_ISOLATED_2_SLOPED_ROOF: isolated 2 sloped roof(2 sloped canopy) (toiture isolee a 2 versants)
  • 5 - CG_LOADAREA_PANEL: Panel or sign
  • 6 - CG_LOADAREA_FOR_SCAFFOLDING: all the linear elements that are inside or intersecting the closed enveloppe with this type of windwalls are considered to be part of scaffolding, com tower, ...
  • 7 - CG_LOADAREA_SHED_VERTICAL_ROOF: shed vertical roof used in monopitch sheds or for 75 degrees < roofs < 80 degrees
  • 8 - CG_LOADAREA_AWNING: awning(auvent) roof
  • 9 - CG_LOADAREA_VAULTED_ROOFS_AND_DOMES: circular load area (windwall) for circular roofs, vaulted roofs, domes, ...
  • 11 - CG_LOADAREA_LOWER_ANGLE_INCLINED_WALL: 45.1 < lower angle inclined wall < GR_MIN_VERTICAL_WALL_ANGLE(75)
  • 12 - CG_LOADAREA_FREE_STANDING_WALL: free standing wall

automaticMesh.description:
Enable automatic mesh

Default value : true

meshType.description:
Meshing style of the element. Three options are available:

- Complete: performs a complete meshing of the element by the global mesh algorithm, defined in the Mesh options dialog box

- Triangulation: creates a triangular meshing with nodes on the sides of the planar element, using the global mesh algorithm

- None: The element is not meshed, the nodes are created at each vertex

Default value : PlanarMeshType.complete

meshDensity.description:
Selecting a method for defining mesh density. The following options are available:

- Global: the mesh element density is defined according to the global mesh settings

- Simplified: define the number of meshes on x and y local axis directions

- Detailed: define the meshing for each edge of the planar element

- Element size: define the size of a mesh element

Default value : PlanarMeshDensity.global
